assertions.reason: The public telemetry API created observable spans for successful and failing operations, including a real ssh.executeCommand call, with span names plus startTime/endTime captured in the in-memory test surface.

observations: validation.success.span captured status ok with startTime and endTime.
ssh.executeCommand captured status ok for hostname and status error for the non-zero exit scenario.
validation.parent.failure also closed with status error and recorded endTime.

assertions.reason: A traced operation created through withSpan exposed host, operation, and exitCode together on one exported span, and the real ssh.executeCommand span also carried host and exitCode attributes.

observations: validation.success.span attributes included host=shuvtest, operation="echo ok", exitCode=0, durationMs=0.
ssh.executeCommand attributes included host=shuvtest, exitCode=0, port=22, ssh.connect_timeout_ms=5000.

assertions.reason: Wrapping ssh.executeCommand inside a parent withSpan produced parent-child linkage across the operation boundary: the child ssh.executeCommand span recorded parentSpanId equal to the parent spanId in both success and failure runs.

observations: The exported CollectedSpan surface did not include trace_id, so propagation was verified via observable parent-child linkage.

assertions.reason: The live layer targeted http://localhost:4318 and a live traced operation completed, but the accessible read-only collector surface only exposed GET / -> 404 and GET /v1/traces -> 405 (POST-only). No readable collector/UI surface was available to prove that an emitted span was actually ingested or visible, so export could not be conclusively verified.

observations: A live traced operation returned ok without surfacing export diagnostics.
collectorRoot.status was 404 and collectorTracesGet.status was 405 with a POST-only message.

assertions.reason: A real ssh.executeCommand failure (remote exit 7) produced error spans with status=error and exception details on both the child and parent traced operations.

observations: The failing ssh.executeCommand span recorded status=error, exitCode=7, and an error string containing telemetry-error.
The surfaced error object had _tag=CommandFailed with host=shuvtest and exitCode=7.
validation.parent.failure also recorded status=error and the propagated error message.

frictions.description: shuvtest SSH responses include the known post-quantum key exchange warning on stderr, so stderr-based observations had to distinguish that pre-existing warning from the intentional telemetry-error line.
frictions.resolution: Used the known-warning context from mission guidance and validated the intentional stderr content separately.

frictions.description: The exported CollectedSpan user surface did not expose trace_id, limiting context-propagation evidence to parentSpanId linkage.
frictions.resolution: Validated propagation by nesting spans around ssh.executeCommand and matching child parentSpanId to the parent spanId in the captured output.

blockers.description: No readable collector endpoint or dashboard surface was available from the assigned tooling to confirm that a live-emitted span reached the collector/UI; the only accessible read-only endpoints were GET / (404) and GET /v1/traces (405 POST-only).
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Ran the live telemetry layer against the default collector URL and performed read-only GET checks on http://localhost:4318/ and http://localhost:4318/v1/traces.

summary: Tested 5 telemetry assertions through the terminal-driven package API surface: 4 passed (VAL-INFRA-011/012/013/015), 1 blocked (VAL-INFRA-014) because collector ingestion could not be proven from the available read-only surface.
